title: Tangy Syrian Lentil Stir-fry
description: A tangy, crispy dish featuring brown lentils with a low-sodium twist and hints of Middle Eastern flavors.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Rinse lentils and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onions and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in carrots and bell pepper, cooking for an additional 3-4 minutes until vegetables soften slightly.
Reserve some of the cooked vegetables on a plate. Add lentils to the skillet with any leftover vegetables from the wok.
Pour in low-sodium vegetable broth or water to cover the lentils, then add salt, pepper, and your preferred tangy sauce (such as lemon juice, vinegar, and spices like cumin and paprika).
Simmer for about 10 minutes until lentils are tender. If using a wok, mix in reserved vegetables with the lentils.
Transfer to an oven-safe dish. Broil under preheated broiler for 2-3 minutes until edges of the mixture turn golden brown and crispy.
